title: erigeron pacayensis
intro: Erigeron pacayensis, also known as Pacay, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Erigeron pacayensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Erigeron pacayensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
